meta.title: Renewal Rush
meta.genre: Web 3D Signal Triage arcade — Three.js FPS, embeddable
meta.tone: Fast, witty, enterprise-playful — not cringe, not heavy
meta.oneLiner: Connect your stack. Surface every signal — market to CRM. Deploy before renewal day.
meta.scope: Small — premium browser 3D FPS, embeddable on quivly.ai. Distribution = URL, not install.
meta.platformNote: Research June 2026: Three.js is the best fit for Renewal Rush — tiny embed load, URL distribution, AI-friendly, WebGPU-ready. See data/web-engine-research.json. Unreal/Unity/Godot web exports are too heavy for marketing embeds.

landingPage.headline: Your new post-sales team, without the headcount.
landingPage.subheadline: AI agents that surface and act on churn risks and expansion signals to grow your accounts on autopilot.
landingPage.tagline: The AI workforce for post-sales teams

enterpriseGames.name: PagerTron (incident.io)
enterpriseGames.why: Tiny vibe-coded game on a marketing page — proves craft, fun, shareable

enterpriseGames.name: Product-themed arcade (SaaS gamification)
enterpriseGames.why: Teach the product metaphor through play, not a demo deck

whatWeAreNot: Full game / multiple levels / story campaign

premiseResearch.conclusion: Best B2B marketing games are browser-based, one mechanic, 30–90 seconds, leaderboard — not 3D engines.

benchmarks.name: PagerTron
benchmarks.sourceQuote: Escape the pager duty — Your pager has a paging problem. The tool that wakes you up... breaks. Press Spacebar to Start. VIEW LEADERBOARD.
benchmarks.mechanic: Retro arcade — Press Spacebar to Start; single-button play with high-score leaderboard
benchmarks.story: Escape the pager duty. Your pager has a paging problem — the tool that wakes you up breaks.
benchmarks.whyItWorks: incident.io blog: vibe-coded in Claude Code; simple game with database of high scores on Anthropic landing page

benchmarks.name: Wizlympics
benchmarks.sourceQuote: Use the Wiz Sensor to avoid fake cloud services, like Amazon Prime Rib, and collect real cloud services, like AWS S3. Collecting real services earns 10 points; a fake service results in game over. Hit 500 points to win swag.
benchmarks.mechanic: Collect real cloud services, avoid fakes — one fake = game over; 10 points per correct collect
benchmarks.story: Cloud Security Olympic Games — distinguish real vs fake cloud services with the Wiz Sensor.
benchmarks.whyItWorks: Product metaphor in one sentence (real vs fake cloud); 500 pts unlocks swag form; infinite retry

benchmarks.name: Unicorn Startup Simulator
benchmarks.sourceQuote: Create a Billion Dollar Start-Up or Die Trying. Your goal is to have a valuation of at least $1 billion by the end of the year. Use your time wisely! Also, make sure to keep your employees happy :)
benchmarks.mechanic: Weekly binary decisions (Okay / Nope) — track valuation and employee happiness
benchmarks.story: Create a Billion Dollar Start-Up or Die Trying — reach $1B valuation in one year while keeping employees happy.
benchmarks.whyItWorks: Score = valuation ($); time-management metaphor from Toggl marketing; shareable high score

benchmarks.name: Renewal Rush (Unreal FPS)
benchmarks.sourceQuote: arena-discovery.json: 139 level actors on Lvl_ArenaShooter; studio-state phase loop-polish-pass-2-complete
benchmarks.mechanic: First-person arena, walk + Agent Pulse beam, spawn waves (prior direction)
benchmarks.story: Customer Command arena — tag at-risk account orbs before timer hits zero (prior direction).
benchmarks.whyItStruggles: 139 actors per arena-discovery.json; blueprints + MCP polish — high build cost vs browser embed for 90s demo

pattern: End card with CTA + share screenshot
Embeddable on marketing site — no download

options.title: Signal Triage
options.subtitle: Whack-a-Risk
options.mechanic: At-risk account cards pop up — click/tap to Deploy Agent. Healthy = ignore. Expansion = bonus. Miss at-risk = churn tick.
options.story: Stack scattered across tools. Quivly Connect pipes CRM, calls, revenue, support, and market signals into one profile — then you triage what surfaces.
options.oneLiner: Connect your stack. Surface every signal — market to CRM. Deploy before renewal day.

productMapping.connect: Stack rail fills (CRM, calls, revenue, support, market) — each pipe unlocks new signal chips on cards
productMapping.see: Unified Customer 360 card: account + ARR + health + source chips showing which integrations surfaced the risk
productMapping.score: Health color + multi-source badge — 2+ chips = Full Stack Signal (higher ARR on deploy)
productMapping.act: Tap deploy → draft grounded in the surfaced signals ('Draft ready — grounded in Gong + Stripe')

options.whyWinner: PagerTron-level simplicity; visceral post-sales metaphor; teaches Act step instantly; booth-friendly on any laptop

options.title: Renewal Run
options.subtitle: Endless runner
options.mechanic: Account health bar shrinks as you run renewal runway. Tap to Act (boost health). Dodge noise signals. Collect expansion orbs.
options.story: Renewal runway is shrinking. Every action buys ARR. Miss one, churn wins.
options.oneLiner: Run the renewal runway — act fast or lose the quarter.

productMapping.act: Tap = deploy agent pulse, bar refills

options.whyNotFirst: Fun but less unique — runner genre is crowded; harder to show account-specific detail

options.title: Next Best Action
options.subtitle: Priority picker
options.mechanic: 3 account cards appear. Pick the highest-priority at-risk account before timer expires. Streak for combo multiplier.
options.story: Quivly ranked your queue. Can you triage as fast as the AI?
options.oneLiner: Beat the clock — pick the account that needs you most.

productMapping.score: Health + risk level on each card

options.whyNotFirst: Most faithful to Quivly Actions Feed but less arcade energy — better as round 2 variant

options.title: Signal Sort
options.subtitle: Connect → See → Score → Act lanes
options.mechanic: Falling signal cards — sort into four lanes matching product flow. Wrong lane = penalty.
options.story: Your stack is noisy. Sort every signal before renewal day.
options.oneLiner: Every signal. One place. Sort fast.
options.whyNotFirst: Teaches full product flow but four lanes may confuse booth players in first 5 seconds

justification.summary: Signal Triage beats alternatives on simplicity, metaphor clarity, and booth readiness — same bar as PagerTron and Wizlympics.
justification.vsRenewalRun: Runner genre is crowded; hard to show per-account ARR, health score, and usage delta on a scrolling HUD.
justification.vsNextBestAction: Faithful to Actions Feed but reads as a quiz, not an arcade — better as phase-2 variant after core loop ships.
justification.vsSignalSort: Teaches full Connect→See→Score→Act but four lanes overload first-time players; fails the 5-second metaphor test.
justification.vsUnrealFps: 139-actor arena + blueprints + MCP polish is high build cost for a 90s marketing embed; browser ships faster and embeds on quivly.ai.
justification.productFit: Whack-a-Risk + stack rail gamifies Connect→Act: integrations unlock signal chips, multi-source cards teach 'Quivly sees more when your stack is connected', deploy mirrors draft grounded in signals.

selectedPremise.title: Signal Triage
selectedPremise.subtitle: Whack-a-Risk
selectedPremise.oneLiner: Connect your stack. Surface every signal — market to CRM. Deploy before renewal day.

arc.copy: Stack scattered? Watch pipes light up — Salesforce, Gong, Stripe, Zendesk, Slack, Market.
Signals you never saw alone now surface on one card — CRM dip + support spike + market layoffs, unified.
Tap Full Stack Signal cards for max ARR — Quivly drafts the save grounded in every source.
Stack coverage % + signals surfaced + rank — 'Every signal. One place. → quivly.ai'

selectedPremise.protagonist: Post-sales lead with a scattered stack — Quivly Connect makes every signal visible
selectedPremise.setting: Quivly command board with live stack rail (left) feeding unified signal queue (center)
selectedPremise.conflict: Siloed tools hide churn until renewal week; only connected stack surfaces the real risks
selectedPremise.stakes: Save ARR before timer hits zero — miss a multi-source signal = bigger churn tick
selectedPremise.resolution: Quivly ingests stack end-to-end, scores health, drafts action grounded in the signals that surfaced it

productMapping.connect: Stack rail animates — pipe CRM, usage, revenue, calls, support, market data into one profile
productMapping.see: Customer 360 card with timeline chips: which source surfaced usage decline, support spike, market event
productMapping.score: Health border + source chip count — market signal chip (purple) boosts priority
productMapping.act: Deploy → '+ARR Saved' + 'Draft ready — grounded in [Gong + Stripe + Market]'

signalStack.headline: Every signal. One place. — gamified end-to-end
signalStack.problem: Post-sales signals live in silos. CRM says healthy. Usage says otherwise. Market news hits LinkedIn before your CSM sees it.
signalStack.quivlyAnswer: Quivly Connect pipes your entire post-sales stack into one live profile per account — then surfaces churn and expansion signals you'd miss in any single tool.
signalStack.landingAnchor: Quivly ingests signals from your entire post-sales stack and autonomously acts on churn risks and expansion opportunities.

integrations.name: Gong
integrations.unlocks: call sentiment, champion risk, competitor mentions

integrations.name: Stripe
integrations.unlocks: payment decline, expansion MRR, overdue invoices

integrations.name: Zendesk
integrations.unlocks: ticket spike, CSAT drop, escalation pattern

integrations.name: Slack
integrations.unlocks: internal churn chatter, exec sponsor gone quiet

integrations.name: Market Signals
integrations.unlocks: layoffs, funding, job changes, competitor wins — external context CRM never has

connectPhase.mechanic: Stack rail fills left-to-right; each integration lights indigo and unlocks its chip type on incoming cards
connectPhase.withoutQuivly: First 3s show grey silo icons — only single-source cards spawn (easy to miss real risk)
connectPhase.withQuivly: After connect, multi-source and market-signal cards appear — teaches 'more integrations = more signal surface area'

cardTypes.example: Gong sentiment down + Zendesk ticket spike
CRM renewal overdue + usage -42% + market layoffs
LinkedIn: champion left + usage decline — purple Market chip

surfacePhase.priorityRule: Deploy red cards with most chips first — mirrors Quivly ranking next best action from combined signals

gamification.whyItMakesQuivlyStronger: Player feels the before/after: siloed stack = blind spots; connected stack = risks surface earlier with context — exactly Quivly's Connect→See→Score→Act promise

premise.hook: Renewal week. Your stack is scattered — CRM, usage, support, market signals in different tools. Quivly Connect pulls it together. Now the risks surface.
premise.protagonist: Player = post-sales lead who only sees siloed signals until Quivly unifies the stack.
premise.setting: Quivly command board — stack rail (left) pipes into unified signal queue (center).
premise.conflict: Single-source view misses churn; multi-source Full Stack Signals need fast deploy before renewal.
premise.stakes: Save ARR before timer hits zero — miss a market-boosted signal = bigger churn penalty.
premise.resolution: Quivly surfaces signals end-to-end (integrated data + external market) and drafts the save grounded in every source.
premise.whyItMatters: Gamifies Quivly's moat: more connected stack → more signals surfaced → faster action with context.
storyBeats: Connect: Stack rail lights up — Salesforce, Gong, Stripe, Zendesk, Slack, Market pipe in
Surface: Cards pop with source chips + health score — 1 chip (silo blind spot) vs 3+ chips (Full Stack Signal)
Deploy: Tap red multi-chip cards — '+ARR' + 'Draft ready — grounded in Gong + Market'
Close: Stack coverage % + signals surfaced + rank + 'Every signal. One place. → quivly.ai'

previousDirection.note: Unreal FPS arena — kept for learning, not primary ship target
previousDirection.hook: Renewal day is in 90 seconds. Accounts are slipping in Customer Command arena.

pillars: One mechanic: tap to deploy — but cards teach stack → surface → act
Product metaphor in 5 seconds: scattered stack → connected → signals surface
Score = ARR saved + signals surfaced + stack coverage %
Shareable end card — 'I connected 6 sources and saved $X ARR'
Browser-first; Unreal stays as Game Lab learning sandbox

phases.name: connect
phases.action: Stack rail fills — 6 integrations light up sequentially

phases.name: surface-and-act
phases.action: Cards pop with source chips; tap at-risk to deploy

steps: 0-10s Connect phase — stack icons pipe into Quivly (grey silos → indigo connected)
10s+ Cards pop with 1-4 source chips (CRM, Calls, Revenue, Support, Comms, Market)
🟢 Healthy single-source — ignore | 🔴 At-risk — tap Deploy | 🟡 Expansion — bonus ARR
🔴+2 chips = Full Stack Signal — 2× ARR | 🔴+Market chip = 2.5× ARR (external signal boost)
Deploy = indigo pulse + '+ARR' + 'Draft ready — grounded in [sources]'
Miss multi-chip at-risk = double churn tick (you had the signal, missed the act)
Speed ramps every 15s as more integrations stay connected
End screen: ARR saved, signals surfaced count, stack coverage %, rank title

acts.title: The only act
acts.description: One board, one loop, replay for high score. Marketing CTA after play.

scenes.title: Signal Triage Board
beats: Title: QUIVLY — Renewal Rush · Every signal. One place.
Connect: stack rail animates — 6 source icons light indigo
Surface: cards pop with chip row (CRM/Calls/Revenue/Support/Comms/Market)
HUD: timer + ARR saved + signals surfaced + stack coverage ring
End card: stack coverage % + ARR + rank + Book a demo CTA

characters.name: You
characters.role: Post-sales lead with Quivly agents on standby

characters.name: Quivly Agent
characters.role: Deploy pulse on tap — indigo flash that saves the account and surfaces the draft

uiCopy.start: Connect your stack. 90 seconds to surface every signal.
uiCopy.startAlt: Every signal. One place. From market to CRM — act before renewal day.
uiCopy.winAlt: ARR secured — your post-sales team, without the headcount.
uiCopy.loseAlt: Risk got there first. Quivly would've had the draft ready.
uiCopy.cta: See how Quivly does this for real → quivly.ai

marketingUse.team: Internal leaderboard, Friday fun, onboarding icebreaker
marketingUse.customers: Booth demo, webinar opener, 'feel the speed' moment

primary: Stack rail component: 6 integrations light up in Connect phase (0-10s)
Account card: ARR, health, usage delta + source chip row (CRM/Calls/Revenue/Support/Comms/Market)
Spawn logic: chip count scales with elapsed time; market chips after stack connected
Score: ARR + signals surfaced + stack coverage % + rank titles
End screen + share screenshot + quivly.ai CTA
phase2: Sound: soft chime on deploy (PagerTron-style optional music toggle)
unrealSandbox: Keep Lvl_ArenaShooter polish as Unreal learning exercise
Do not block marketing ship on Unreal/WebGL export

openQuestions: Leaderboard: local only first, or web/API from day one?
Swag unlock at score threshold (Wizlympics-style) or pure bragging rights?
lockedDecisions: Premise: Signal Triage + Stack Connect narrative (confirmed)
Platform: browser-first — Game Lab canvas → quivly.ai embed (confirmed)
Unreal FPS arena: learning sandbox only, not marketing ship target (confirmed)
Priority: premium dark SaaS look — Stripe meets arcade glow, not military shooter
Interaction: tap Deploy — indigo pulse feedback, not rifle / gun fantasy
Brand synced from quivly.ai landing page — forest logo, indigo accent #6366F1

visualDirection.aesthetic: Premium dark SaaS command center — Stripe meets arcade glow
visualDirection.mood: Calm confidence, fast but never chaotic

gameplayMapping.connect: Left stack rail — Salesforce, Gong, Stripe, Zendesk, Slack, Market animate into Quivly hub
gameplayMapping.see: Customer 360 card with chip row showing which sources surfaced the risk
gameplayMapping.score: Health border + chip count; purple Market chip = external signal priority
gameplayMapping.act: Deploy on multi-chip cards — draft cites sources ('grounded in Gong + Stripe + Market')

world.board: Split layout — stack rail (left 20%) + 3×3 card slots (center) + HUD (top)
world.stackRail: Vertical pipe of 6 source icons; grey → indigo glow as each connects
world.background: #0A0A0A with indigo pulse along connect pipes when stack goes live
world.cards: Glass tiles — company initial, ARR pill, health badge, chip row, usage sparkline
world.chips: Tiny rounded badges per source; Market chip uses purple accent; 3+ chips = gold Full Stack border
world.spawn: Cards rise from slot; early spawns = 1 chip (silo), late spawns = 2-4 chips (connected stack)

feel.input: Single click/tap — no movement, no aim
feel.feedback: Indigo deploy pulse, soft chime, floating +ARR text, brief card shrink on save
feel.motion: Subtle slot pulse on spawn; speed ramp every 15s
feel.ui: Minimal HUD — thin font, glass panels, big renewal timer, streak counter

avoid: Gun models, muzzle flash, blood, industrial grey blocks
Loud arcade chaos, cheap particle spam

unrealPolishChecklist: Enable Lumen + Virtual Shadow Maps in project settings
Add Post Process Volume: bloom, exposure, slight vignette
Replace rifle with invisible Agent Pulse (line trace + Niagara beam)
Account orbs: emissive materials + point lights
UMG HUD: Quivly colors, Inter-style typography feel
Target 60fps — small arena, few actors = smooth on Mac
